e_visa.notes: Online visitor visa applications can be submitted through the official ISS eServices portal at eservices.iss.gov.gy. Visa types include Visitor, Business, Employment, and Student. This is an online pre-approval system rather than a traditional e-visa; applicants receive a reference number to check status. The portal returned HTTP 403 on re-fetch June 2026 — the URL is known from prior validated data.

visa_policy.notes: Visa-free list sourced primarily from Guyana Ministry of Foreign Affairs (minfor.gov.gy/visa-information), re-verified June 2026. Secondary source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium (embassyofguyana.be). Guyana's immigration eServices portal (eservices.iss.gov.gy) handles online visa applications. Nationals of countries not on the visa-free list must apply for a visa at a Guyana embassy/consulate or, if no diplomatic presence is accessible, apply online for a visa approval letter (treated as visa-on-arrival upon presentation). Countries listed where only diplomatic/official/service passports qualify are noted in conditional_access, NOT visa_free.

conditional_access.conditions: Guatemalan nationals holding a US Permanent Resident Card may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Guatemala for 90 days for holders of Diplomatic/Official Passports and holders of an US Permanent Resident Card or a valid US/Canadian/Schengen Visa'.

Guatemalan nationals holding a valid US visa may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Guatemala for 90 days for holders of Diplomatic/Official Passports and holders of an US Permanent Resident Card or a valid US/Canadian/Schengen Visa'.

Guatemalan nationals holding a valid Canadian visa may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Guatemala for 90 days for holders of Diplomatic/Official Passports and holders of an US Permanent Resident Card or a valid US/Canadian/Schengen Visa'.

Guatemalan nationals holding a valid Schengen visa may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Guatemala for 90 days for holders of Diplomatic/Official Passports and holders of an US Permanent Resident Card or a valid US/Canadian/Schengen Visa'.

Honduran nationals holding a US Permanent Resident Card may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium verbatim: '90 days holders of a US Permanent Resident Card or a valid US/Canadian/Schengen Visa'. NOT independently confirmed on minfor.gov.gy — Honduras does not appear in the enumerated MFA list.
Honduran nationals holding a valid US visa may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium. NOT independently confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.
Honduran nationals holding a valid Canadian visa may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium. NOT independently confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.
Honduran nationals holding a valid Schengen visa may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium. NOT independently confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.

Mexican ordinary passport holders with a US Permanent Resident Card may enter visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium. Note: minfor.gov.gy lists Mexico only for diplomatic/official/service passport holders — ordinary-passport credential access is NOT confirmed on the primary MFA source.
Mexican ordinary passport holders with a valid US visa may enter visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium. NOT confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.
Mexican ordinary passport holders with a valid Canadian visa may enter visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium. NOT confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.
Mexican ordinary passport holders with a valid Schengen visa may enter visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium. NOT confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.

Guatemalan diplomatic and official passport holders enter visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Guatemala for 90 days for holders of Diplomatic/Official Passports and holders of an US Permanent Resident Card or a valid US/Canadian/Schengen Visa'.
Cuban diplomatic, official and service passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'Cuba for holders of Diplomatic/Official/Service Passports'.
Venezuelan diplomatic, official and service passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'Venezuela for holders of Diplomatic/Official/Service Passports'.
Bolivian diplomatic, service and official passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'Bolivia for holders of Diplomatic/Service/Official Passports'.
Uruguayan diplomatic and official passport holders enter visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Uruguay for holders of Diplomatic/Official Passports for 90 days'.
Haitian diplomatic passport holders only. MFA verbatim: 'Haitians for holders of Diplomatic Passport'.
Chinese diplomatic and service passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'China (People's Republic of) for holders of Diplomatic/Service Passports'.
Cypriot diplomatic and service passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'Cyprus for holders of Diplomatic/Service Passports'.

Indonesian diplomatic and service passport holders enter visa-free for up to 30 days. MFA verbatim: 'Indonesia for 30 days for holders of Diplomatic/Service Passports'. Note: Embassy of Guyana Belgium lists Indonesia without passport-type restriction (ordinary passports 30 days) — minfor.gov.gy (primary source) restricts this to diplomatic/service passports only.
Israeli diplomatic and service passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'Israel for holders of Diplomatic/Service Passports'.

Indian diplomatic, official and service passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'India for holders of Diplomatic/Official/Service Passports'. OCI cardholders are NOT covered — no official Guyana source mentions OCI as a qualifying credential.
Kuwaiti diplomatic and service passport holders enter visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Kuwait for holders of Diplomatic/Service Passports for 90 days'.
Turkish diplomatic, service and special passport holders enter visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Türkiye for holders of Diplomatic/Service/Special Passports for 90 days'.

Mexican diplomatic, official and service passport holders enter visa-free for up to 90 days (unconditional, by passport type alone). MFA verbatim: 'Mexico for 90 days for holders of Diplomatic/Official/Service Passports'.
Georgian diplomatic passport holders only. MFA verbatim: 'Georgia for holders of Diplomatic Passports'.
Spanish diplomatic passport holders only. MFA verbatim: 'Spain for holders of Diplomatic passports'.

outbound_official.notes: Guyana Ministry of Foreign Affairs (minfor.gov.gy/visa-information) publishes two lists: (1) who may enter Guyana without a visa, and (2) countries for which Guyanese citizens do not require a visa. The outbound list below is sourced from list (2). Entries restricted to diplomatic/official/service passports only are excluded from visa_free and visa_on_arrival — they apply only to non-ordinary passport holders. Cuba (dipl/official/service only), Bolivia (dipl/service/official only), Venezuela (dipl/official/service only), Uruguay (dipl/official only), Mexico (dipl/official/service only), Guatemala (dipl/official or with US/CA/Schengen visa/PR), China (dipl/service only), Cyprus (dipl/service only), Indonesia (dipl/service only), Israel (dipl/service only), Kuwait (dipl/service only), Turkey (dipl/service/special only), Georgia (dipl only), Spain (dipl only), Switzerland (dipl/service for official short-term events only), India (dipl/official/service only) are NOT listed here as they do not apply to ordinary-passport Guyanese citizens. Page last updated: October 16, 2025.

cbi.notes: No official Citizenship by Investment program exists in Guyana. No draft law or official program announcement has been found on any government domain. Some aggregator/marketing sites claim a program exists but no official government source confirms this.

programs.name: Employment/Investor Visa
programs.notes: Guyana offers an employment visa for foreign investors/businesspersons. Initial visa can be obtained on arrival; work permit valid up to 3 years, renewable. No minimum investment amount is specified on official government pages. No formal 'golden visa' or dedicated residence-by-investment program exists. Path to permanent residency or citizenship timelines not specified in official materials.

programs.name: CARICOM Free Movement of Skills (CSME)
programs.eligibility: CARICOM nationals who qualify as skilled workers: university graduates (bachelor's or equivalent including ACCA), musicians and artistes, sports persons, media workers, registered nurses, trained teachers, artisans with Caribbean Vocational Qualifications, associate degree holders. Also covers self-employed and service providers.
programs.notes: Under the CARICOM Single Market and Economy (CSME) / Revised Treaty of Chaguaramas. Qualified CARICOM nationals can move and work in Guyana without a work permit. A Free Movement of Skills Certificate is issued. Fee in Guyana: G$1,500.00. Participating nations: Antigua and Barbuda, Barbados, Belize, Dominica, Grenada, Guyana, Jamaica, St. Lucia, St. Kitts and Nevis, St. Vincent and the Grenadines, Suriname, Trinidad and Tobago. (Bahamas, Montserrat, Haiti excluded.)

agreements: CARICOM Single Market and Economy (CSME) - Free Movement of Skilled Nationals

validation_notes: Re-validated against minfor.gov.gy and embassyofguyana.be on 2026-06-14.
CORRECTED: North Korea (PRK) source updated — confirmed present on minfor.gov.gy (primary MFA), not just Belgium embassy as previously noted.

CORRECTED: Cuba, Venezuela, Bolivia, Haiti, Uruguay, Mexico, Georgia, Spain were already in conditional_access in the prior record. Split verified: Cuba/Venezuela/Bolivia had been incorrectly grouped as a single conditional_access entry; now separated into individual country entries for Bolivia, Venezuela, Cuba, and Haiti for clarity and correctness per MFA verbatim text.

CORRECTED: Guatemala conditional_access updated — prior record used 'eligible_nationalities: Guatemala' grouping but also had an incorrect combined passport_type entry for 'Guatemala, Honduras'. Now separated: Guatemala diplomatic/official entry is its own conditional_access entry sourced from minfor.gov.gy; Honduras entries remain sourced only from Belgium embassy.

CORRECTED: Indonesia — minfor.gov.gy clearly restricts to diplomatic/service passports only (30 days). Belgium embassy suggests ordinary passports allowed. MFA is authoritative; Indonesia moved firmly into conditional_access (passport_type basis). Indonesia was already in conditional_access; added note about discrepancy.
CORRECTED: Panama stay limit — minfor.gov.gy states 90 days; Belgium embassy states 180 days. Primary MFA source used (90 days). Note added to visa_free entry.

CORRECTED: Prior record had a combined conditional_access entry for 'Guatemala and Honduras' for diplomatic/official passports — now separated into Guatemala-only entry (confirmed on MFA) since Honduras does not appear on minfor.gov.gy at all.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, Peru as ordinary-passport 90-day visa-free — all confirmed present on minfor.gov.gy.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: All CARICOM members visa-free with no limit — confirmed.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: North America (Canada, USA) visa-free no limit — confirmed.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED: European countries (Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Finland, France, Greece, Ireland, Italy, Luxembourg, New Zealand, Norway, Portugal, Sweden, Switzerland, Netherlands, UK, Albania) — all confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED: Russia (90 days), Hong Kong (90 days), Maldives (30 days), Qatar (30 days), Korea (30 days), Singapore (30 days), UAE (90 days), Botswana, Ghana (90 days), South Africa (30 days) — all confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.
UNVERIFIED: Germany — not in the enumerated minfor.gov.gy list on re-fetch but present on Belgium embassy page. Retained with Belgium embassy source.
UNVERIFIED: Malaysia (60 days) — not in the enumerated minfor.gov.gy list on re-fetch but present on Belgium embassy. Retained with Belgium embassy source.
NOTE: Japan is listed on minfor.gov.gy with no conditional restriction and no stay limit.
NOTE: Australia listed explicitly on minfor.gov.gy — confirmed, no limit.
gaps: Exact maximum stay duration not specified for several CARICOM visa-free nationals on the official MFA page (listed as no limit)
The Embassy of Guyana Belgium domain (embassyofguyana.be) is an official embassy site but not a .gov.gy domain — data from it has been included only where it corroborates or extends the MFA list
North Korea listed on both minfor.gov.gy and Belgium embassy — unusual and may be outdated; included with minfor.gov.gy as source
Germany listed on Belgium embassy page only (not in the enumerated country list on minfor.gov.gy re-fetch); may be listed in MFA introductory text only — needs direct verification
Malaysia (60 days) listed on Belgium embassy page only — not in enumerated minfor.gov.gy list on re-fetch; status uncertain
Honduras conditional credential access: sourced ONLY from Belgium embassy — Honduras does not appear in any enumerated entry on minfor.gov.gy; may be outdated or only apply conditionally

Mexico ordinary passport credential access: sourced ONLY from Belgium embassy — minfor.gov.gy lists Mexico only for diplomatic/official/service passports; discrepancy may reflect page update lag or a separate bilateral agreement not published on primary MFA page
Lesotho (14 days) appears on Belgium embassy page but was not included in the current record — insufficient confirmation from primary MFA source
Bangladesh diplomatic/official/special passports (30 days) appears on Belgium embassy page but not in the current record — insufficient confirmation from primary MFA source
Aruba, Cayman Islands, Turks & Caicos Islands listed on Belgium embassy page — omitted as these are territories, not independent states, and their status on official MFA page is unclear
Indonesia discrepancy: Belgium embassy implies ordinary passports (30 days); minfor.gov.gy restricts to diplomatic/service passports only — MFA is authoritative
Panama stay discrepancy: Belgium embassy says 180 days; minfor.gov.gy says 90 days — MFA used (90 days)
No official e-visa in the traditional sense; eservices.iss.gov.gy portal returned HTTP 403 on re-fetch — the URL is known from prior validated data and ISS is the official immigration system
No digital nomad, startup, or fast-track talent visa scheme found on official sources
No official CBI or dedicated golden visa/RBI program found on official government sources
Minimum investment amount for investor/employment visa not specified in official materials
Path to permanent residency timeline not officially published
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder conditional access found on any official Guyana government source
No bilateral visa waiver/suppression agreement dates found in official sources for diplomatic passport waivers — dates not published on minfor.gov.gy
Cape Verde diplomatic/service passport conditional access appears on Belgium embassy page but not confirmed on minfor.gov.gy — not included in this record
